name: ArcticRuff Dropship
goal:
Build an automated dropshipping business for fur hood replacements (brand: ArcticRuff). The frontend is already deployed at arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app (Next.js 16, Tailwind v4). The business model: customers order on our English site, orders get relayed to Chinese Taobao suppliers who ship directly to buyers internationally. NO inventory held.

BUILD THESE SYSTEMS:

1. ORDER PIPELINE: Customer checkout → order stored in Supabase → auto-formatted into Chinese (supplier speaks NO English) → sent to supplier via WeChat API or email. Include: buyer shipping address, product variant (color/pattern/size), quantity. Order status tracking (placed/confirmed/shipped/delivered).

2. SUPPLIER INVENTORY SYNC: Build a scraper/sync system that pulls product data from Taobao supplier listings. Extract: available colors, patterns, sizes, prices (CNY), stock status, shipping options. Store in Supabase. The storefront should only show in-stock variants. Run sync on a schedule (daily cron or n8n webhook).

3. CHECKOUT & PAYMENTS: Stripe checkout page at /checkout with the 3 tiers ($149/$199/$299 CAD). Collect shipping address, jacket model, fur color/size preference. Store orders in Supabase with payment status.

4. ADMIN DASHBOARD: /admin page showing: all orders with status, supplier inventory snapshot, revenue stats, ability to manually update order status, export to CSV. Basic auth protection.

5. BILINGUAL ORDER RELAY: When an order comes in, auto-generate a Chinese-language order summary (产品/颜色/尺寸/收货地址) formatted for copy-paste into WeChat or auto-send. Include international shipping address transliterated to Chinese format.

6. NOTIFICATION SYSTEM: Slack webhook for new orders + email confirmation to buyer via Resend.

7. SEO + ANALYTICS: Proper meta tags, JSON-LD Product schema, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, GA4 with event tracking.

Tech: Next.js App Router, Supabase (DB + auth), Stripe, Resend, Tailwind CSS v4. All API keys via env vars.

name: Pawcasso Atelier
goal: Improve the Pawcasso Atelier website (pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app) — an AI-generated animal portrait e-commerce site built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The site sells custom AI pet portraits for $9. Focus on these priorities: 1) IMAGE OPTIMIZATION: Convert all gallery PNGs (currently 5-8MB each, 14 images in website/public/gallery/) to WebP format, implement Next.js Image component with proper srcSet/sizes/lazy loading throughout the gallery, add blur placeholder data URLs. 2) SEO & META: Add Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags to all pages, add JSON-LD structured data (Product schema on order page, ImageGallery on gallery), create sitemap.xml and robots.txt, add canonical URLs. 3) MOBILE & UX: Add skeleton loading states for the gallery grid, improve mobile responsiveness of gallery filters and order form, add smooth image loading transitions. 4) ORDER FORM UPGRADE: Add a pet photo upload field to the order form at website/src/app/order/page.tsx so customers can upload a photo of their pet — store the uploaded file using Vercel Blob or base64 in the form submission. The existing tech stack: Next.js 14 app router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Stripe checkout. Gallery data is in website/src/lib/data.ts. Images are in website/public/gallery/.

name: Japan Trip Companion
goal: Build the ultimate Japan trip companion web app for a 2-week cherry blossom trip (Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, Nara). The existing site is at ~/japan-trip/ with an interactive Leaflet map and day-by-day sidebar. Enhance it into a world-class travel guide with: (1) Interactive maps with walking/transit route directions between stops, estimated travel times, and Google Maps deep links. (2) Trilingual support — toggle between English, Chinese (Simplified), and Japanese for place names, descriptions, and key phrases. (3) A 'What's Next' mode that highlights the current/next activity based on time of day. (4) Useful Japanese travel phrases with pronunciation for each context (restaurant, train, temple). (5) Restaurant reservation details, confirmation numbers, and direct links. (6) Cherry blossom forecast integration. (7) Offline-friendly PWA with service worker so it works without internet in Japan. (8) Weather widget showing forecast for each day. (9) A packing/checklist feature with local storage persistence. (10) Mobile-first responsive design optimized for using on-the-go.

name: ArcticRuff Commerce
goal: Build the full ArcticRuff e-commerce business. The frontend website is already deployed at arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app (Next.js 16, Tailwind CSS v4, deployed to Vercel). Now build: (1) Waitlist backend using Supabase — store email, name, jacket model, fur color preference, timestamp. Create Supabase project and wire up the existing WaitlistForm.tsx to submit real data via API route. (2) Email confirmation flow — send a branded confirmation email when someone joins the waitlist using Resend or similar. (3) Admin dashboard page at /admin — shows all waitlist entries, export to CSV, basic stats. (4) Stripe checkout integration — add a /checkout page with Stripe Elements for actual payment processing (// CAD tiers). (5) SEO optimization — add proper meta tags, structured data (JSON-LD Product schema), sitemap.xml, robots.txt. (6) Google Analytics 4 setup with event tracking for waitlist signups and checkout. (7) Connect the waitlist form submission to send a Slack/Discord notification for new signups.

name: TaxBridge
goal: Build TaxBridge MVP — a US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Bridge web app for the core scenario: H-1B/TN visa tech workers with US RSUs who now live in Canada. MVP features: (1) RSU vesting entry with date, FMV, shares, employer (Meta/Amazon/Google/Microsoft), (2) Dual-country tax calculator showing US federal+state and Canada federal+provincial tax on RSU income using treaty Article XV, (3) Foreign Tax Credit optimizer to avoid double taxation, (4) Required forms checklist (W-2, 1040/1040-NR, T1, T4, FBAR, 8938, 8833), (5) USD/CAD conversion at Bank of Canada rates, (6) Dashboard with filing status. Tech: Next.js App Router, TailwindCSS, TypeScript, SQLite via better-sqlite3. Modern fintech UI.
